description:
You are logged into a workstation as the security-engineer whose home directory is /home/user.  
    Your team rotates the service account’s SSH credentials every January.  
    The previous key is already stored in /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys and is labelled
        old-key-2023.
    
    Goal  
    1. Generate a brand-new ED25519 key-pair using ssh-keygen.  
       • Private key absolute path:  /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_rotate  
       • Public  key absolute path:  /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_rotate.pub  
       • Key comment (-C option):    svc-rotate@example.com  
       • Passphrase:                leave EMPTY (-N "")  
       • The command must run non-interactively (use appropriate flags).  

    2. Update /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys so that:  
       a) The old key line that ends with the comment old-key-2023 is **commented out** by
          prefixing a single “# ” (hash + one space).  
       b) The existing deployment-key line must remain unchanged.  
       c) Your newly generated public key is appended as the very last line and
          must contain the comment svc-rotate@example.com.  
       After editing, authorized_keys has exactly three non-blank lines, in this order:  
          • The commented-out old key (starts with “# ssh-ed25519 … old-key-2023”)  
          • The deployment key (starts with “ssh-rsa … deployment-key”)  
          • The new key you just generated (starts with “ssh-ed25519 … svc-rotate@example.com”)  
       Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline.

    3. Create a rotation audit log file at /home/user/rotation_logs/2024-ssh-key-rotation.log  
       • First make the directory /home/user/rotation_logs if it does not exist.  
       • Capture the timestamp of when the key was generated (local system time) and the SHA256
         fingerprint that ssh-keygen displays for the new public key.  
       • The log file must contain exactly TWO lines in the precise format below
         (note the colon placement and single spaces):  
             Rotation Timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
             New Key Fingerprint: SHA256:<base64string>  
         Replace the date, time and <base64string> with the real values you obtain.  
         End the file with a single trailing newline character.  

    4. File/directory permissions must respect standard OpenSSH defaults:  
       • /home/user/.ssh            : 700  
       • All key files and authorized_keys : 600  
       • /home/user/rotation_logs   : 700  
       • /home/user/rotation_logs/2024-ssh-key-rotation.log : 600  

    What the automated checker will look for  
       • /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_rotate and
         /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_rotate.pub both exist.  
       • /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys contains the three lines in the order described,
         where the final line ends with “svc-rotate@example.com”.  
       • The log file exists, has the exact two-line format shown above, and the second line
         contains the substring “SHA256:”.  
       • All paths and permissions match the specification.

    Important  
       • Do NOT delete any existing files.  
       • Use only the Linux terminal; no manual file uploads.  
       • Remember to create the rotation log exactly as specified so that pattern-based
         automated tests pass.  
       • You have regular user privileges; do not use sudo.  
       • Do not output additional commentary that would be written into any of the files.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the task starts:

    1. Directory /home/user/.ssh already exists with mode 700.

    2. /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys initially contains **exactly** the two lines below
       (with a single trailing newline at the end of the file):

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIDSgcf5xgxN0R8vG5YkNVrYJv1nVbWcv1O3MH8b6XAQ1 old-key-2023
ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAABAQC4an1j2xy7b3Dx3XcX8GpXxFfXriYrAYx0QHoQOH9d6QhvzXnYFho7ozatajIuHQw6mbeid8X8oI6INBnNw+9xCLdfDUbqMf95xTeNwCvufqJgHE4vvAJ8NjgrZpTr6lYQkPzwHC27bMqf+AKeFEg3UhYg2uscd/lXFk4Isllwz6TQ1iXwRXy2U1UXoCM deployment-key

    3. No other files are present in /home/user/.ssh.
       No directory /home/user/rotation_logs yet.

    Expected state after successful completion:

    1. /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_rotate  (mode 600)  – private key, content unpredictable.
    2. /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_rotate.pub  (mode 600) – public key whose
       last field (comment) is “svc-rotate@example.com”.
    3. /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys  (mode 600) now contains EXACTLY:

# ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIDSgcf5xgxN0R8vG5YkNVrYJv1nVbWcv1O3MH8b6XAQ1 old-key-2023
ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAABAQC4an1j2xy7b3Dx3XcX8GpXxFfXriYrAYx0QHoQOH9d6QhvzXnYFho7ozatajIuHQw6mbeid8X8oI6INBnNw+9xCLdfDUbqMf95xTeNwCvufqJgHE4vvAJ8NjgrZpTr6lYQkPzwHC27bMqf+AKeFEg3UhYg2uscd/lXFk4Isllwz6TQ1iXwRXy2U1UXoCM deployment-key
ssh-ed25519 <PUBLIC_KEY_BYTES> svc-rotate@example.com

       where <PUBLIC_KEY_BYTES> is any valid base64 string representing the new key.

    4. Directory /home/user/rotation_logs exists with mode 700.

    5. /home/user/rotation_logs/2024-ssh-key-rotation.log exists with mode 600 and
       contains exactly two lines, each ending with a newline:

Rotation Timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
New Key Fingerprint: SHA256:<base64fingerprint>

       The first line’s timestamp must be syntactically valid for the regex
       [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}
       and the second line must start with “New Key Fingerprint: SHA256:”.

    6. Permissions for /home/user/.ssh stay at 700; for each new file 600; for rotation_logs 700.

    The automated checker will:
       • Verify file existence and permissions with stat.  
       • Check the three-line structure and order inside authorized_keys.  
       • Confirm that the last line of authorized_keys ends with “svc-rotate@example.com”.  
       • Validate that the log file has exactly two lines and the second includes “SHA256:”.
